00038 
00039 package java.lang;
00040 
00053 public final class Math
00054 {
00058     private Math()
00059     {
00060     }
00061 
00066     public static final double E = 2.718281828459045;
00067 
00073     public static final double PI = 3.141592653589793;
00074 
00083     public static native double sin(double a);
00084 
00092     public static native double cos(double a);
00093 
00102     public static native double tan(double a);
00103     
00110     public static native double log(double a);
00111 
00118     public static native double exp(double a);
00119 
00127     public static native double pow(double a, double b);
00128 
00138     public static double toRadians(double degrees)
00139     {
00140         return (degrees * PI) / 180;
00141     }
00142 
00152     public static double toDegrees(double rads)
00153     {
00154         return (rads * 180) / PI;
00155     }
00156 
00168     public static native double sqrt(double a);
00169 
00179     public static native double ceil(double a);
00180 
00189     public static native double floor(double a);
00190 
00205     public static int abs(int i)
00206     {
00207         return (i < 0) ? -i : i;
00208     }
00209 
00224     public static long abs(long l)
00225     {
00226         return (l < 0) ? -l : l;
00227     }
00228 
00240     public static float abs(float f)
00241     {
00242         return (f <= 0) ? 0 - f : f;
00243     }
00244 
00256     public static double abs(double d)
00257     {
00258         return (d <= 0) ? 0 - d : d;
00259     }
00260 
00268     public static int max(int a, int b)
00269     {
00270         return (a > b) ? a : b;
00271     }
00272 
00280     public static long max(long a, long b)
00281     {
00282         return (a > b) ? a : b;
00283     }
00284 
00293     public static float max(float a, float b)
00294     {
00295         // this check for NaN, from JLS 15.21.1, saves a method call
00296         if (a != a)
00297             return a;
00298     
00299         // no need to check if b is NaN; > will work correctly
00300         // recall that -0.0 == 0.0, but [+-]0.0 - [+-]0.0 behaves special
00301 
00302         if (a == 0 && b == 0)
00303             return a - -b;
00304 
00305         return (a > b) ? a : b;
00306     }
00307 
00316     public static double max(double a, double b)
00317     {
00318         // this check for NaN, from JLS 15.21.1, saves a method call
00319         if (a != a)
00320             return a;
00321     
00322         // no need to check if b is NaN; > will work correctly
00323         // recall that -0.0 == 0.0, but [+-]0.0 - [+-]0.0 behaves special
00324 
00325         if (a == 0 && b == 0)
00326             return a - -b;
00327 
00328         return (a > b) ? a : b;
00329     }
00330 
00338     public static int min(int a, int b)
00339     {
00340         return (a < b) ? a : b;
00341     }
00342 
00350     public static long min(long a, long b)
00351     {
00352         return (a < b) ? a : b;
00353     }
00354 
00363     public static float min(float a, float b)
00364     {
00365         // this check for NaN, from JLS 15.21.1, saves a method call
00366         if (a != a)
00367             return a;
00368     
00369         // no need to check if b is NaN; < will work correctly
00370         // recall that -0.0 == 0.0, but [+-]0.0 - [+-]0.0 behaves special
00371         if (a == 0 && b == 0)
00372             return -(-a - b);
00373     
00374         return (a < b) ? a : b;
00375     }
00376 
00385     public static double min(double a, double b)
00386     {
00387         // this check for NaN, from JLS 15.21.1, saves a method call
00388         if (a != a)
00389             return a;
00390     
00391         // no need to check if b is NaN; < will work correctly
00392         // recall that -0.0 == 0.0, but [+-]0.0 - [+-]0.0 behaves special
00393         if (a == 0 && b == 0)
00394             return -(-a - b);
00395     
00396         return (a < b) ? a : b;
00397     }
00398 }
